title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross of New Hampshire State Police passed away unexpectedly at age 41, authorities announced.
Ross served 19 years in various roles including Major Crime Unit and led Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit.
A fund for his family has been set up; public services are scheduled for July 6 and 7 in Hampton.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized Celtics' communication after being traded to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown expressed dissatisfaction with Brad Stevens' facilitation of trade conversations.
Celtics traded Brown for Paul George and multiple draft picks despite not requesting a trade.

title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 76-year-old Hull man, Robert Nolan, died after an apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach on Wednesday evening.
Plymouth County District Attorney's office announced Nolan was found unresponsive in the water and pronounced dead at 8:40 p.m.
The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner took custody of Nolan's body; no foul play is suspected, authorities say.

title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins trade goalie Joonas Korpisalo to Rangers for cap relief and acquire defenseman Will Borgen from New York.
Boston signs Connor Clifton and extends Jordan Harris to strengthen the defense during NHL free agency.
Bruins retain $5.3 million in cap space but may make additional roster changes before the season starts.

title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Parking fees waived at all Massachusetts state parks and beaches July 3-5 for Fourth of July weekend.
Waiver honors the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution, announced by Gov. Maura Healey.
High-demand locations may close if parking reaches capacity; plan visits via DCR Park Alerts webpage.

title: What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets?
author: John Paul
bullets: Gasoline-saving gadgets like pills, air filters, and magnets are not effective, according to AAA Northeast's Car Doctor.
Run-flat tires have improved but still provide a firmer ride and higher replacement costs compared to conventional tires.
A 2002 Honda Accord with 90,000 miles should have its timing belt replaced due to its age, despite low mileage.

title: Boston civilian board recommends changes to BPD policies on bodycam footage, federal entanglements
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The Office of Police Accountability and Transparency recommends releasing Boston police bodycam footage within 30 days of critical incidents.
OPAT suggests Boston police end collaborations with federal agencies, citing risks to civil rights and community trust.
The memo calls for tighter controls on biometric data sharing and increased transparency regarding federal partnerships.

title: Senators seek to restrict social media use for minors, target ‘most addictive features’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts Senate proposes a bill to disable certain social media features for minors to enhance safety and mental health.
The bill avoids requiring government ID for age verification, tasking the attorney general's office with setting age verification methods.
Despite support, concerns persist over potential impacts on civil liberties and marginalized communities, especially LGBTQ+ youth.

title: Isaiah Thomas comes to Jaylen Brown’s defense after shocking Celtics trade​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded from the Celtics to the 76ers for Paul George and draft picks.
Former Celtics player Isaiah Thomas defended Brown's character amidst criticism following the trade.
Brown expressed shock over the trade and gratitude for his time with the Celtics and their fans.

title: Jeremiah Poope takes us on the Freedom Trail for America’s 250th (yes, that’s really his name)
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jeremiah Poope, a longtime Boston Freedom Trail guide, donned period costume despite near-100-degree temperatures for America's 250th anniversary.
Poope's unique name and storytelling style engage students, making historical tours lively with personal anecdotes and legendary tales.
He became a tour guide in 2003, drawn by the job after a conversation revealed pay was significantly higher than his prior work.
